creator: Ted Anderson
brief: The first known recursive acronym in computing history — an editor for MagicSix at MIT.

creator: MIT Lisp Machine team
brief: MIT Lisp Machine editor inspired by TINT. 'EINE' is German for 'one' — a multi-layered linguistic joke.

creator: MIT Lisp Machine team
brief: The successor to EINE. 'ZWEI Was EINE Initially' is both a recursive acronym and a German bilingual pun.

creator: Ted Anderson
brief: Ted Anderson's retort to EINE/ZWEI. Possibly a pattern-play that never saw full implementation.

creator: Ted Anderson
brief: The second offering from TINT's creator: another editor that 'is not TECO either'.

creator: Richard Stallman
brief: The most famous recursive acronym in history. The foundation of the free software movement.

creator: GNE project team
brief: An early Wikipedia competitor that explicitly followed GNU's naming template.

creator: Rasmus Lerdorf
brief: The most widely-used example of backronymization: 'Personal Home Page' → 'PHP: Hypertext Preprocessor'.

creator: Clark Evans
brief: Originally 'Yet Another Markup Language', later redefined with 'Ain't'. Uses vernacular negation.

creator: Bob Amstadt, Eric Youngdale
brief: Originally 'WINdows Emulator', redefined recursively to make a precise technical distinction.

creator: Erik Troan, Marc Ewing
brief: Originally 'Red Hat Package Manager', redefined recursively after community adoption. Debated status.

creator: XBMC Foundation
brief: Originally 'Xbox Media Center', redefined when the project moved beyond Xbox. Now known as 'Kodi'.

creator: University of Washington
brief: 'PINE Is Nearly Elm' — a recursive name that uses 'Nearly' instead of 'Not', expressing partial derivation.

creator: GNU nano team
brief: The earliest version name of GNU nano: 'TIP isn't Pico'. A recursive jab at its predecessor.

creator: Joseph H. Allen
brief: One of the few recursive acronyms without negation. Expresses ownership rather than differentiation.

creator: Mike Cheng
brief: Uses 'Ain't' for vernacular denial. The name is a legal-technical claim: LAME distributes source code, not binaries.

creator: PNG Development Group
brief: Official name: 'Portable Network Graphics'. Unofficial recursive expansion: 'PNG's not GIF'. Both coexist.

creator: Ian Bicking
brief: The Python package installer. 'PIP Installs Packages' — functional recursion without negation.

creator: Daniel Stenberg
brief: Multi-level recursion: C = cURL, U = URL. Two recursive letters in one acronym — extremely rare.

creator: MIT TECO community
brief: The oldest known recursive backronym (1960). 'Mash Until No Good' → 'Mung Until No Good'.

creator: Paul Davis
brief: Functional recursion: descriptive rather than humorous. Represents the mature form.

creator: Douglas Comer
brief: Academic OS from Purdue. 'Xinu' = 'Unix' reversed, then recursively expanded.

creator: Free Software Foundation
brief: A Freemacs derivative. Similar name to MiNT but entirely different project — deliberate confusion?

creator: Gwydion Dylan team
brief: 'MINDY Is Not Dylan Yet' — uses 'Yet' to imply eventual convergence. Temporal recursive negation.

creator: Christian Bauer et al.
brief: Precise technical distinction: it's a C++ library for CAS, not a standalone CAS.

creator: Shawn Hargreaves
brief: Game development library. Earlier Atari version: 'Atari Low Level Game Routines'. Platform-independent now.

creator: Aaron Digulla et al.
brief: Originally 'Amiga Research Operating System'. Rebranded for legal independence from Amiga trademark.

creator: Fiji contributors
brief: Uses 'Just' for minimalism: FIJI IS 'just' ImageJ — humble positioning through recursive form.

full_expansion: LiVES is a Video Editing System
creator: Gabriel Finch
brief: Purely descriptive recursion. The name IS the definition: what it IS, not what it ISN'T.

full_expansion: Nagios Ain't Gonna Insist On Sainthood
creator: Ethan Galstad
brief: The longest and most linguistically elaborate recursive expansion: 7 words. Greek wordplay.

creator: NiL team
brief: A Liero-like game. 'NiL' means 'zero/nothing' — an abstract recursive self-negation.

full_expansion: Ninja-IDE Is Not Just Another IDE
creator: Ninja-IDE team
brief: 'Not Just Another IDE': recursive denial embedded in the N.I.H. (Not Invented Here) anti-pattern territory.

full_expansion: SPARQL Protocol And RDF Query Language
creator: W3C
brief: W3C standard. The recursive form became an official standards body naming convention.

creator: Google
brief: Google's recursive naming for a modern RPC framework. Corporate adoption of hacker convention.

creator: Isaac Schlueter
brief: Meta-recursive: the expansion denies being an acronym WHILE being a recursive acronym. Grelling-Nelson paradox territory.

creator: Ondrej Filip et al.
brief: Functional recursive naming in network infrastructure. The 'BIRD' metaphor adds a poetic layer.

creator: David Roundy
brief: A version control system. The 'Darcs' spelling is a creative respelling of 'Darcs' as 'David's ARCS'.

creator: University of Illinois at Chicago
brief: VR environment. Metaphorical ('cave') + recursive naming. Academic origin.

creator: William M. Shubert
brief: An online Go server. Minimal recursive form: name IS definition.

creator: Till Tantau
brief: German recursive acronym: 'TikZ is not a drawing program'. Cross-linguistic evidence.

creator: Romain Lievin
brief: Texas Instruments calculator linking utility. Functional recursive naming.

full_expansion: TRESOR Runs Encryption Securely Outside RAM
creator: TRESOR team
brief: AES encryption patch. 'TRESOR' = German/French for 'treasure/vault' — metaphor + recursion.

creator: Nokia
brief: Uses periods between letters — only recursive acronym with this typographic style.

creator: Unknown
brief: Self-refuting recursion: 'Isn't a Recursive Acronym' — but it IS one. Meta-commentary on the tradition.

creator: Unknown
brief: The positive counterpart to UIRA: 'IS a Recursive Acronym'. Self-affirming recursion.

creator: Victor M. Alvarez
brief: Self-aware recursion: explicitly acknowledges being 'Another Recursive Acronym' in its own expansion.

creator: Zinf team
brief: Fork of FreeAmp audio player. Classic 'Is Not' fork-denial recursive naming.

full_expansion: Hurd of Unix-Replacing Daemons / Hird of Interfaces Representing Depth
creator: Thomas Bushnell
brief: The only well-documented mutually recursive acronym pair. Hurd↔Hird. Standing as a linguistic curiosity.

creator: Michael Tiemann et al.
brief: First commercial recursive acronym: a company name. 'Cygnus' = constellation Cygnus (Swan).

creator: K.Y. Ho et al.
brief: Graphics hardware company. Recursion embedded in the standard 'Company Name Inc.' corporate form.

creator: Dee Hock
brief: Possibly the earliest recursive acronym overall (1976). A global brand name that most users don't realize is recursive.

creator: Svenska Aeroplan AB
brief: Swedish automotive company. Originally non-recursive: 'Svenska Aeroplan AB' → recursive backronym 'SAAB Automobile AB'.

creator: British West Indian Airways
brief: Caribbean airline. Decolonization naming: 'British West Indian Airways' → recursive 'BWIA West Indies Airways'.

creator: Kim Dotcom
brief: Cloud storage service. Successor to Megaupload. 'MEGA' as hyperbolic branding + recursive form.

creator: HIM International Music
brief: Taiwanese independent record label. Recursive naming outside Western hacker culture.

creator: Portuguese government
brief: Portuguese national airline. Originally 'Transportes Aereos Portugueses', later recursive backronym.

creator: Government of India
brief: Indian state oil company. Debatable: 'Oil' is a common noun — is the recursion intentional or coincidental?

creator: Scott Nash
brief: American organic grocery chain. 'MOM' as familial branding + recursive corporate name.

creator: UCSF
brief: Free chemical compound database. 'ZINC' = Zinc element + recursive declaration of open access.

creator: HQS Quantum Simulations
brief: German quantum computing startup. Recursive naming in cutting-edge tech, not retro hacker humor.

creator: GPE project
brief: Linux-based palmtop environment. Functional recursive naming for mobile GUIs.

creator: Apache Friends
brief: Web server stack. Recursive acronym embedded in a multi-component list — hybrid form.

creator: MATE team
brief: GNOME 2 fork. 'MATE' = South American tea (yerba mate) + recursive technical description.

creator: NANOGrav collaboration
brief: High-precision pulsar timing software. Scientific computing adopts hacker naming convention.

creator: SCInterface team
brief: Server control interface. Triple recurrence: SC in SCInterface, SCInterface Control Interface.

creator: Douglas Hofstadter
brief: First use of 'recursive acronym' in print. Hofstadter's Godel, Escher, Bach (1979).

creator: John Brunner
brief: The earliest known recursive acronym in fiction. 'Stand on Zanzibar' (1968).

creator: Scott Adams
brief: Dilbert comic strip: the recursive naming convention enters mainstream pop culture awareness.

full_expansion: Guitar Rock Utilizing Nihilist Grunge Energy
creator: The Simpsons writers
brief: The only known recursive acronym where the recursive letter is NEITHER first NOR last (5th of 6 letters).

creator: David Lynch, Mark Frost
brief: Twin Peaks antagonist. 'Beware of BOB' — recursive warning that contains its own subject.

full_expansion: Kosmos Obey Strategical Multiple Operation System
creator: Monolith Soft
brief: Xenosaga game series character. Japanese game development adopts recursive naming convention.

creator: Sun Microsystems
brief: First 'recursive anti-acronym': 'Jini Is Not Initials' — it denies being what it is.

creator: Microsoft
brief: Microsoft game framework. 'XNA's Not Acronymed' — a meta-joke by a major corporation.

creator: Mazda / car enthusiast community
brief: Mazda MX-5 Miata. Community-generated recursive backronym: 'Miata Is Always The Answer'.

full_expansion: Smart Programmers Are Required to Know SPARKS
creator: Ellis Horowitz, Sartaj Sahni
brief: Tail-recursive acronym. The recursive letter is at the END: 'Smart Programmers Are Required to Know SPARKS'.

creator: Unknown
brief: Rare tail-recursive form: recursion on the LAST letter. 'Your Own Personal YOPY'.

full_expansion: Hijos por la Identidad y la Justicia contra el Olvido y el Silencio
creator: Argentine human rights organization
brief: Spanish recursive acronym. Human rights organization: 'Sons and Daughters for Identity and Justice...'

creator: Benoît Minisini
brief: A BASIC-like language. Uses 'Almost' — partial identity rather than denial.

creator: Mark of the Unicorn
brief: 'MINCE is Not Complete Emacs' — another early MIT-era recursive editor name.

creator: MICO team
brief: CORBA implementation. Positive recursive identification: 'MICO IS CORBA'.

creator: Apple Inc.
brief: macOS/iOS kernel. 'X is Not Unix' — the single-letter 'X' representing the entire kernel name.

full_expansion: ENAML is Not A Markup Language
creator: ENAML project
brief: Recently created recursive acronym. Directly echoes YAML's negation pattern.

creator: MIT Scheme team
creator_affiliation: MIT Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory
brief: A recursive acronym for a Scheme-to-C compiler developed at MIT. LIAR was part of the MIT Scheme implementation and is notable for naming itself after the function it performs — applying recursive compilation through self-reference.

creator: MIT/LLNL researchers
creator_affiliation: MIT and Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory
brief: A data analysis and scientific computing language developed at LLNL. MINC positions itself through negation — it draws on C-like syntax but extends it for interactive data processing, hence 'Is Not C'.

creator: Jasta and contributors
brief: A modular peer-to-peer file sharing daemon that acts as a backend for multiple P2P protocols. The name is a recursive homophonic acronym — 'gift' as in something freely given, fitting for open-source file sharing.

full_expansion: KINACS is not a circuit simulator
creator: University research group
brief: A circuit analysis tool that disclaims being a circuit simulator through its own name — ironic self-reference common in the hacker naming tradition.

creator: Open source developers
brief: An open-source 2D/3D game engine. The recursive name signals its position as an alternative to mainstream commercial engines.

creator: Unix sysadmin community
brief: A Unix account management utility with a recursive name that directly describes its function.

creator: MusicBrainz / Relatable
brief: The acoustic fingerprinting technology behind MusicBrainz. The recursive name elegantly encodes what the system does — it 'recognizes music'.

creator: Computer music researchers
brief: A Lisp-based environment for computer music composition and synthesis. YLEM (an ancient Greek term for primordial substance) describes itself recursively.

creator: Indonesian Linux community
brief: An intelligent firewall system from the Indonesian open-source community. Notable as one of the few recursive acronyms originating outside the US/Europe.

full_expansion: GEECS for Electronics Engineering Computers and Science
creator: University department
brief: An academic department's recursive acronym covering Electronics Engineering, Computers, and Science — demonstrating that recursive naming extends into institutional contexts.

creator: José Cláudio Faria
brief: A Windows code editor that explicitly positions itself against Microsoft Notepad. The double negation (Tinn Is Not Notepad) mirrors the recursive pattern of TINT (TINT Is Not TECO).

creator: Hacker community
brief: A meta-recursive joke: an acronym that recursively declares itself NOT to be an acronym. This is recursive self-reference at the level of naming ontology.

creator: Xinha community
brief: A WYSIWYG HTML editor that forked from HTMLArea, encoding its fork lineage in a recursive negation acronym.

creator: Open source developers
brief: A Windows API compatibility layer that follows WINE's naming pattern: declaring it is 'not an emulator' in its very name. Part of a family of recursive-negation compatibility tools.

creator: X11 community
brief: A vector graphics editor for X11. The recursive name is refreshingly positive — 'Fig Is Good' — in contrast to the more common negation-based recursive acronyms.

creator: East Asian open source community
brief: A Japanese input method editor with a recursive name. One of the very few recursive acronyms originating in the East Asian open-source community.

creator: Unix hacker community
brief: The most famous joke recursive backronym in computing. During the editor wars, EMACS was humorously re-interpreted as a recursive acronym mocking its resource consumption.

full_expansion: WINAMP Is Not An MP3 Player
creator: Hacker community
brief: A joke recursive backronym for the famous audio player. Winamp was originally 'Windows AMP' (Advanced Multimedia Products), but the recursive reinterpretation became widespread hacker folklore.

creator: Bandung Linux community
brief: The Bandung Linux Club in Indonesia. 'Klub' is the Indonesian spelling of 'club', making this a homophonic recursive acronym where the word and abbreviation are near-synonyms.

creator: Polish Linux developers
brief: A Polish-origin Linux distribution with a recursive name. One of the few recursive acronyms from Eastern European open-source communities.

creator: GES founders
brief: A major trade show and convention services company. The recursive name uses homophonic recursion — GES describes itself as GES.

creator: ADAP founders
brief: A US auto parts retailer chain with a recursive acronym. Homophonic recursion where the name and abbreviation are semantically identical.

creator: PTT Public Company Limited
brief: Thailand's state-owned oil and gas exploration company. The acronym PTTEP recursively contains 'PTT', making it one of the few recursive corporate acronyms from Southeast Asia.

creator: UK Home Office
brief: The UK's biometric immigration system. The name 'Iris' and the acronym 'IRIS' are homophonic, with the word 'iris' referring to both the eye's structure and the recognition technology.

creator: Human rights organizations
brief: An anti-human trafficking organization. The word 'STOP' and its recursive expansion are semantically aligned — a rare case where homophonic recursion serves an imperative function.

creator: Mary James
brief: An international support organization for at-home mothers. The recursive acronym encodes the group's purpose — 'Moms Offering Moms Support' — in its own name.

full_expansion: Respect, Equality, Socialism, Peace, Environmentalism, Community, and Trade unionism
creator: George Galloway and others
brief: A UK political party whose name recursively spells out its founding values. The most elaborate semantic recursive acronym in political branding.

creator: UK banking consortium
brief: The UK's ATM interchange network. The recursive acronym creates a double meaning: both the network and the verb describing its function.

creator: Independent wrestling promoters
brief: An independent professional wrestling promotion. The recursive name 'New Era Wrestling' signals its identity as a new generation of wrestling entertainment.

creator: Adrienne (mother)
brief: A human name intentionally created as a recursive acronym. LIAS is the only known case of a person being named with a recursive acronym at birth.

creator: Frank B. Brokken
brief: A Unix documentation formatting language. The recursive name plays on 'yodel' while describing the tool's purpose — online documentation processing.

creator: Tech community (unofficial backronym)
brief: The most famous commercial recursive backronym: 'Bing Is Not Google'. A community-created joke acronym for Microsoft's search engine that spread through tech culture within hours of Bing's launch.
